title: How does a vague grilling question become answerable by a teammate?
summary: The developer keeps ownership while the coding agent packages business context, the current recommendation, and one precise question for a teammate.

options.label: Copy a contextual ask
options.description: Packages the exact gap, business context, current recommendation, and visual so the teammate can answer confidently.

options.label: Send only the question
options.description: Fast, but the teammate must reconstruct the feature and why the answer matters.

options.label: Keep investigating alone
options.description: Useful only while repo or documentation evidence remains available locally.

participants.label: Developer
participants.description: Owns the decision and missing context

participants.label: Coding agent
participants.description: Runs Grill Me and prepares the ask

messages.label: Ask the next precise question
messages.description: Use the native question dialogue with the recommended answer first.

messages.label: I do not have enough context
messages.description: The developer chooses the teammate escalation abbreviation.

messages.label: Render current understanding
messages.description: Only settled, non-secret context enters the visual artifact.

messages.label: Return local diagram
messages.description: The self-contained page stays local unless sharing is explicit.

messages.label: Copy ask packet
messages.description: Clipboard text includes context, recommendation, exact question, and diagram reference.

messages.label: Paste one confident ask
messages.description: The teammate receives enough business and technical context to answer directly.

messages.label: Return company context
messages.description: The decision owner brings the answer back into the grilling session.
